lede: A look at March 1 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Frédéric Chopin and Juan Bernat, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: English Wikipedia
events.desc: English-language Wikipedia reaches its one millionth article, Jordanhill railway station.

events.title: Bosnia and Herzegovina
events.desc: Bosnia and Herzegovina declares its independence from Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Bulgaria signs the Tripartite Pact, allying itself with the Axis powers.

events.title: Australia national cricket team
events.desc: The Australian cricket team captained by Warwick Armstrong becomes the first team to complete a whitewash of The Ashes, something that would not be repeated for 86 years.

events.title: Saint Petersburg
events.desc: Following mass protests in Petrograd demanding greater freedom in the RSFSR, the Kronstadt rebellion begins, with sailors and citizens taking up arms against the Bolsheviks.

events.title: Battle of Adwa
events.desc: Battle of Adwa: An Ethiopian army defeats an outnumbered Italian force, ending the First Italo-Ethiopian War.

events.title: Yellowstone National Park
events.desc: Yellowstone National Park is established as the world's first national park.

events.title: Napoleon
events.desc: Napoleon returns to France from his banishment on Elba.

events.title: Samuel Chase
events.desc: Justice Samuel Chase is acquitted at the end of his impeachment trial before the U.S. Senate.

births.name: Yitzhak Rabin
births.line: Israeli general and politician, 5th Prime Minister of Israel, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: João Goulart
births.line: Brazilian lawyer and politician, 24th President of Brazil

deaths.text: Georges J. F. Köhler, German biologist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Stephen II of Hungary, king of Hungary and Croatia

observances.text: Beer Day, marked the end of beer prohibition in 1989 (Iceland)
Christian feast day: Agnes Tsao Kou Ying (one of the Martyr Saints of China)
